text:
Team GB golfer Charley Hull failed to reach her ‘expected level’ with a poor performance at theOlympicsGames on Wednesday, posting a nine-over 81.

That first-round score put Great Britain’s Hull at the foot of the leaderboard at Le Golf National, in what was a shocking opening performance from the LPGA star.

Speaking ahead of the first round, Hullexpressed fear that her chances of goldin the women’s golf event could be harmed by theParis2024 smoking ban.

Former golfer Trish Johnson, speaking after the first round, is disappointed Hull didn’t reach her ‘expected level’, explaining how it went wrong from the first tee.

‘Charley Hull has not had the best of years,’ Johnson told BBC Radio 5 Live. ‘She has had a few niggly injuries.

‘But she hasn’t really performed to her expected level, especially after last year’s second-place finishes in the majors. It all started on the first tee after she knocked it into the water.’

Hull is adamant her disappointing performance had nothing to do with the fact she couldn’t smoke on the course, despite her comments ahead of the first round.

‘Definitely not,’ Hull said. ‘It’s because I’ve been injured [with my shoulder].

‘I feel a bit rusty, because obviously, it’s been five weeks since I’ve last properly played a four-day event.

‘Hopefully, I’ve got the rust off me and I’m looking forward to hopefully shoot (expletive) 9 under tomorrow.’

Hull, 28, is currently ranked No. 11 in the world rankings and is hoping to secure a second golfing medal for Great Britain.

Tommy Fleetwood clinched silver in the men’s golf event, finishing behind American Scottie Scheffler and ahead of Hideki Matsuyama on the podium.

‘There’s part of me that’s disappointed, of course, but at the same time I never dreamt I would be an Olympic medallist,’ Fleetwood said.

title: Team GB star fails to reach 'expected level' with shocking Olympic performance | Metro News
description: Team GB star Charley Hull failed to reach her 'expected level' with a poor performance at the Olympics on Wednesday, posting a nine-over 81.
